title: Major Trend Reversal Trap Confirmation
focus_questions: Who got trapped, and where did they become objectively wrong rather than merely uncomfortable?
Did confirmation truly close through the breakout side's control level, or is the reversal thesis still premature?

summary.thesis: A high-value major trend reversal comes from trapped breakout traders plus confirmation, not from fading an old trend because it looks stretched.
summary.operator_translation: Do not pick tops and bottoms. Wait until the breakout side loses the exact level they needed and the rescue attempt also fails.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when a trend continuation attempt fails, traps participants, and the market confirms reversal through an objective control level.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: late trend, wedge exhaustion, or failed breakout near a meaningful edge. Required evidence: strong signal side, trap event, confirmation close through the trapped side's control level, and failed rescue. Do not trade: by fading extension alone or before confirmation. Real trigger: confirmation close plus failed rescue by the trapped side. Failure point: the trapped side immediately reclaims control and holds it.

when_relevant: An established trend is trying to continue from a breakout or strong signal bar.
The continuation side becomes trapped near a meaningful edge or late-trend structure.
The operator needs confirmation-focused reversal logic instead of emotional fading.
required_evidence: the continuation side first looks strong enough to trap traders into following
price then closes through the level that objectively invalidates those followers
the first rescue attempt fails and the opposite side starts gaining travel
confirmation_signals: body close through the trapped side's control level
the trapped side cannot rescue on the first meaningful retest
the opposite direction begins to travel with more efficiency than the prior continuation attempt
invalidation_signals: the trapped side quickly reclaims and holds the level that was supposed to invalidate them
the setup was only a normal pullback in a still-dominant trend
the signal side never trapped committed traders in the first place
no_trade_conditions: Do not front-run reversal before trap confirmation exists.
Do not use this logic when the move is still behaving like a normal pullback in a strong trend.
Do not trust a weak trap story with no objective control level violation.

entry_archetypes.name: confirmation close reversal
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the reversal is confirmed by a close through the trapped side's control level.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the market is still inside unresolved chop and confirmation is ambiguous.

entry_archetypes.name: failed rescue retest
entry_archetypes.description: Enter on the first failed rescue attempt by the trapped side after confirmation is already visible.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the trapped side reclaims and stabilizes instead of failing the rescue.

management_notes: Confirmation matters more than calling the exact turning point.
The first failed rescue often offers the cleanest business read after confirmation.
Once trapped-side release is largely spent, late chasing can degrade into balance.
review_questions: Who exactly got trapped, and where did they become objectively wrong?
Was the reversal trade taken after confirmation, or was it just emotional fading of an old trend?
After confirmation, did the trapped side get a rescue chance and still fail?
